questions.question: Define the difference between a discrete random variable and a continuous random variable. Provide an example of each.
questions.hint: A discrete random variable can only take on specific values, whereas a continuous random variable can take on any value within a given range.

questions.question: A random experiment has two possible outcomes: success or failure. The probability of success is 0.6, and the probability of failure is 0.4. Find the expected value of a binomial distribution with n = 3 and p = 0.6.
A continuous random variable X has a probability density function f(x) = 2x for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1. Find the probability that X lies between 0.2 and 0.8.
questions.hint: Use the definite integral to find P(0.2 ≤ X ≤ 0.8)
